English: What is the best time to plant bananas in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī īhinda rīrīkū rīega rīa kūhanda marigū būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: Bananas can be planted throughout the year in Kenya, but the best time is during the rainy season to ensure adequate soil moisture for young plants.
Kikuyu: Marigū no mahandūo mahinda o mothe mwaka-inī, no ihinda rīega makīrīa rīa kuhanda nī rīrīa kūrī na mbura nyingī nīguo gūtigīrīra tīīrī wīna ūgūnyu wa kūigana kūrī mūmera.

English: What temperature do banana require to grow?
Kikuyu: Nī gīthimi kīrīkū kīa ūrigarī kībatiī kūgacīra ūkūria wa marigū?

English: For optimal growth, bananas require a warm humid climate.
Kikuyu: Nīguo gūkūra wega, marigū mabatie rīera ria ūrūgarī na wūigū ūigananīīrie.

English: An average temperature of 20°C-30°C is required.
Kikuyu: Ūrugarī wa kuuma gīthimi kīa 20°C nginya 30°C nī ūbatiī.

English: At Below 20°C, normal plant growth is retarded.
Kikuyu: Ūngīnyihīra gīthimi kīa 20°C, mūmera nī wīgucagia gūkūra

English: Lacatan and Valery tolerates cold weather better than other varieties.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ya marigū ya Lacatan na Valery nī yūmagīrīria kūndū kūrī na heho gūkīra mīthemba īngī.

English: Cooler areas (higher altitudes) slow down plant development and the inflorescence may also fail to emerge.
Kikuyu: Macigīrīra marīa marī na heho(kūndū kūrī igūrū) nīgūtūmaga marigū megucie gūkūra na mahūa nomage kumīra. .

English: What are the ideal climatic conditions for banana farming in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū mega ma rīera mabataiī ūkūria-inī wa marigū būrūri-inī wa Kenya.

English: Bananas thrive in temperatures between 20°C and 30°C and require well-distributed rainfall throughout the year.
Kikuyu: Marigū makūraga wega kūndū kūrī ūrūgarī wa 20°C nginya 30°C na mabataraga mbura īraura wega mwaka-inī wothe.

English: What is the recommended altitude to grow bananas?
Kikuyu: Marigū mabatiī gūkūrio matūra-inī marī kūndu kūrī igūrū atīa?

English: Altitudes of below 1800 m above sea level are generally recommended for the production of bananas..
Kikuyu: Marigū mendekithagio gūkūrio matūra-inī marī mita 1800 gūcoka nathī igūrū wa iria.

English: How much rainfall do banana require?
Kikuyu: marigū mabataranaga mbura īīgana atīa?

English: Bananas can grow well with an annual rainfall of between 1000 and 2500 mm.
Kikuyu: marigū no makūre wega na mbura ya mūigana wa milimita 1000 nginya 2500 o mwaka.

English: optimal yields require a well distributed annual rainfall of 1400 mm or more, without long dry spells.
Kikuyu: Nigūo kūgīa na magetha maingī, marigū mabatie mbura ya mūigana wa 1400mm kana makīria harī mwaka.

English: What are the major challenges faced by banana farmers in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mathīna marīkū magomeire arīmī a marigū Kenya?

English: Challenges include pests, diseases, fluctuating market prices, limited access to credit, and inadequate infrastructure.
Kikuyu: Mathina nī ta: tūtambi, mirīmū, gūcejia kwa mathogora mathoko-inī, kūaga kūheo lūni na kūaga ūthii wa nambere wa matūūra ukīnyanīīre īkīro.

English: Are there government support programs for banana farmers in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī kūrī honge cia thirikari ya Kenya iheyanaga ūteithio kūrī akūria a marigū?

English: Yes, the Kenyan government offers various support programs, including subsidies, training, and access to credit for banana farmers.
Kikuyu: Nī kūrī, thirikari ya Kenya nī īrī honge irīa iteithagia ūkūria wa marigū na njīra cia, kūnyīhīa mathogora, kubūdithia arīmī na kūhe arīmī mbeca cia wonjoria

English: How can I improve the profitability of my banana farming operation?
Kikuyu: Ingīongerera atia baita harī ūkūria wa Marigū

English: Focus on cost-effective practices, market research, and adopting modern farming techniques to enhance profitability.
Kikuyu: Rora makīria njīra cia kūnyihia ngarama, īka ūthuthuria mūiganu, rīma ūkīhūthīra njīra cia kīīrīu nīguo kuongerera baita

English: Are there training programs available for new banana farmers in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī kūrī tabarīra cia ūrutani kūrī arīmī eru a marigū Kenya?

English: Yes, agricultural extension services and training programs are available through government agencies and agricultural organizations to help new banana farmers acquire essential knowledge and skills.
Kikuyu: Nī kūrī tabarīra īcī īria cionekaga kūhetūkira honge cia thirikari cia ūrīmī nīguo gūteithia arīmī eru na ūgi ūria ubatie.

English: What are the economic benefits of growing bananas in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī baita īrīkū cia ūkurīa cionekaga nīundū wa marigū?

English: Banana farming in Kenya is a significant agricultural sector that plays a crucial role in the country’s economy.
Kikuyu: Urīmī wa marigū nī mwega Kenya tondū nīutethagia gūkūria būrūri.

English: Kenya is one of the leading banana-producing countries in Africa, and this industry has provided employment opportunities for many rural communities while also contributing to food security and export revenue.
Kikuyu: Kenya nī ūmwe wa mabūrūrī ma Afrika marīa marī na ūkūria mūnene wa marigū, ūkūria ūyu nī uteithitie kūhingūra mawīra matūra-īnī na kuongerera wūīngi wa īrīo na wendia wa nja ya būrūri.

English: Which county in Kenya produce a lot of bananas?
Kikuyu: Ni County irīkū īkūragia muno marigū Kenya?

English: Kisii region produces the bulk of the cooking varieties, while the central and eastern regions produce more desert varieties.
Kikuyu: Matūra ma Gīthii nīmo makūragia mūno marigū mithemba ya kūruga, nakīo gīcigo gīa gatagatī ka ka būrūri na ithūīro rīa Kenya nīcio cikūragia mūno mithemba ya kwīruhia.

English: Unlike in other East African countries where bananas are cooked as part of the staple diet, Kenyan consumers prefer desert banana.
Kikuyu: Mūgarū na mabūrūrī mangi ma Afrika ya ithūiro, marīa makūragia marigū ma kūruga, arīi a Kenya mendaga marigū meru.

English: The demand for ripened fruit often outsrips supply, which has created opportunities for imports from neighbouring countries.
Kikuyu: Ūbatari wa marigū meru nī ukīraga marīa tūrahota gūkūria, ūndū ūrīa ūtamaga tūgūre kuuma nja ya mabūrūri marīa tūrigainiī

English: Despite its importance, banana production in Kenya has not received due policy attention, unlike other cash and horticultural crops.
Kikuyu: Mūgarū na baita ya mo, ūrīmi wa marigū ndūrī warūmbūiyo na mawatho ta mīmera īria īngī ya biacara na mahūa.

English: Meru County is the largest banana producer, among the banana producing counties in Kenya.
Kikuyu: County ya Meru nīo īri ūciarithania mūnene wa marigū Kenya

English: How profitable is banana farming in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Urīmī wa marigū ūkoragwo na baita īigana atīa?

English: Let us assume that they will be ready for harvesting (as 12 months is the average for most cooking bananas),
Kikuyu: Reke tuge marigū ma kūruga makūraga thutha wa mieri 12,

English: and that you harvest 450 bunches of good size and they will be sold at an average figure of Shs12,000 per bunch (farm gate),
Kikuyu: na ūngīgetha ngoro 450 nene na ciendio gatagatī-inī ka 12,000 o ngoro,

English: you will generate Shs5,400,000 at this first harvest,
Kikuyu: nī uguthodeka shiringī 5,400,000 igetha rīa mbere.

English: How many banana trees per īka in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Īka īmwe īngīhanduo ītīna cigana cia marīgu Kenya?

English: Getting a special breed of bananas which are resistant to pests and diseases is a tall order.
Kikuyu: Kuona mūthemba ūria wītīragia tutambi na mīrimu wīna bata makīria

English: Go for bananas that have undergone tissue culture because they are hybrid kind of and normally do well.
Kikuyu: Etha mīthemba ya gūciarithanio ya kīīrīu tondū nīīciaraga wega.

English: In an īka you can plant approx. 800–1000 bananas.
Kikuyu: Harī īka imwe, no ūhande kuuma ītina 800 nginya 1000

English: Which county produces most bananas in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī county īrīkū īciarithanagia marigū mūno Kenya?

English: Meru County is currently the leading producer of bananas in Kenya, having overtaken Kisii County in the recent past.
Kikuyu: County ya Meru nīo īri ūciarithania mūnene wa marigū Kenya, rīu nī īhetūkite county ya Gīthii.

English: How do you make bananas bigger?
Kikuyu: Īngīnenehia atīa marigū?

English: The trick is to cut the purple flower below the stalk off when the small nectar-filled flowers start to fall off.
Kikuyu: Thiri ni kūrenga kīro kīa purple thī wa mūkori rīrīa mahūa marīa manini mabīrīrīa kūgūa.

English: It is nature's indication that the plant has made all the fruit it can support.
Kikuyu: Ūū nīkuonania mūmera ūyū nīūciarīte matunda ma kūigana

English: The fingers, as the bananas are called, will get big
Kikuyu: Ciara cia marigū nīikūneneha.

English: How long does banana take to grow?
Kikuyu: marigū moyaga kahinda kaigana atīa gūkūra?

English: Bananas do not grow from a seed but from a bulb or rhizome, and it takes 9 to 12 months from sowing a banana bulb to harvesting the fruit.
Kikuyu: marigū matikūraga kūumana na mbeu no nī mathundūkaga, na moyaga kahinda ka mieri 9 nginya 12 kuuma kuhanda nginya kūgetha matunda.

English: The banana flower appears in the sixth or seventh month.
Kikuyu: Kīro kīa marigū kīumagīra mweri-inī wa gatandatū nginya wa mūgwanja.

English: Unlike other fruit like apples which have a growing season, bananas are available all year round.
Kikuyu: Mūgarū na matunda marīa mangī, marigū nī monekaga mwaka-inī wothe

English: What are the challenges facing banana production?
Kikuyu: Nī mathīna marīkū magomagīra ūciarithania wa marigū?

English: Lack of planting materials as a challenge in banana farming.
Kikuyu: Wagi wa indo cia kūhanda nī thina mūnene harī ūrimī wa marigū

English: Conventional banana farming involves using suckers as planting materials.
Kikuyu: ūrīmi wa marigū nī ūhūthagīra thuna ta indo cia bata cia urīmi

English: Due to limited planting materials, most farmers are forced to use suckers from existing orchards.
Kikuyu: Nīūndū wa wagi ūyū, arīmī aingī mahandaga sakasī mīgūnda-inī yao.

English: How many bananas can you get from one tree?
Kikuyu: Hihi ūngīona marigū maigana atīa o harī gītina?

English: As the bud unfolds, it reveals double rows of tiny flowers.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa kīro kīrekūmba, kīambagia kuonania tūhūa tūnini

English: Each of these flowers will become an individual banana, or a “finger.”
Kikuyu: O harī kīro kīu kionanagia matunda maria magūciaruo

English: Each row of bananas is call a “hand” and is made up of 14 to 20 fingers.
Kikuyu: O he raini ītaguo mwahū na o mwahū īthondokaguo nī marigū 14-20

English: Each stem grows 9 to 12 hands, which means that a single banana plant can produce up to 240 bananas.
Kikuyu: Ngoro īmwe īkoragwo na mīahū 9-12 , ūguo nī kuuga īrīgū rīmwe no rīciare marigū 240

English: How long does a banana fruit take to mature?
Kikuyu: ītunda ria īrigu rīoyaga kahinda kaigana atīa gūkūra?

English: Bananas are harvested raw and ripened artificially.
Kikuyu: marigū magethagwo marī methe magacoka makarindwo

English: The dwarf bananas are ready for harvest within 11- 14 months after planting, while tall cultivars take about 14-16 months to harvest.
Kikuyu: marigū mūthemba mūkuhī makūraga thutha wa mīeri 12-14 thutha wa kūhada, namo mūthemba mūraihu woyaga mīeri 14-16 nīguo magethwo

English: A bunch usually takes 90-120 days to mature after shooting, depending on climate and cultural practices.
Kikuyu: Kīhonge kioyaga thikū 90-120 gūkūra thutha wa gūthunduka kūringana na rīera na marīa matūrigicīirie

English: What month is banana season?
Kikuyu: Nī mweri ūrikū wa kīmera kīa marigū?

English: Unlike other fruit like apples which have a growing season, bananas are available all year round.
Kikuyu: Mūgarū na matunda marīa mangī, marigū nī monekaga mwaka-inī wothe

English: Do banana flowers need to be pollinated?
Kikuyu: Kīro kīa marigū ñī kībatiī gūcīarithanio?

English: Bananas are propagated through cuttings which means that all bananas are clones of each other.
Kikuyu: Marigū marīa mothe mahetūraguo, makoroguo mothe mahanaine mwana na nyina.

English: Hybrid bananas are parthenocarpic, which means that they don't need to be pollinated to produce fruits.
Kikuyu: marigū ma kīrīu matibataraga gūcīarithanio nīguo kūgīa matunda

English: Wild bananas do require pollination. They are pollinated by fruit bats.
Kikuyu: marigū ma mūtitū nī mabataranaga gūcīarīthanio. Maciarithanagio nī tunyoni

English: Do you need a male and female banana tree?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī ūbataraga īrigu rīa gīke na njamba?

English: Banana trees do not have a gender in the same way that animals do.
Kikuyu: Mītī ya marigū ndīrī gīke kana njamba

English: However, bananas are produced by banana plants, which are usually propagated through vegetative reproduction, such as by using cuttings from the parent plant.
Kikuyu: marigū maumaga kūrī mīmera ya marigū, marīa kingī maumaga kūrī thuna

English: What is De-suckering?
Kikuyu: De-suckering nī gwīka atīa?

English: Competition between suckers depletes soil fertility very fast and results in weak plants which are very susceptible to infections.
Kikuyu: Macindano ma thuna nīmatūmaga īrīo tīīrī-inī ithire na ūgūo gūtūma mūmera wage gwītiria mīrīmū

English: About 3 to 4 suckers should be maintained per stool in order to ensure strong plants and good yields.
Kikuyu: thuna 3-4 nicio ībatiī kūroruo nīguo gūtīgīrira mūmera nīwagia magetha mega na wīna hinya.

English: Any ex- African Organic Agriculture Training Manual Banana suckers should be removed when they are still young.
Kikuyu: kūringana na Ex African Organic Agricultural Training Manual, thuna cibatie kūeherio cīrī o nini

English: Suckers at different growth stages (mother, daughter and granddaughter) on the opposite side of the mother plant, should be chosen,
Kikuyu: thuna ikūraga na irathi ngūrani ngūrani( maitū, mwarī na gacūcū) mwena-inī wa gītina kīa maitu, nī ho ubatie gūthurwo

English: also to avoid competition for light.
Kikuyu: nigūo kūgīrīrīria gūtharana ūtheri.

English: De-suckering should be done well, so that pruned suckers do not grow up again.
Kikuyu: Kweheria thuna kūbatiī gwīkwo wega nīguo gūtigīrīra ītīkūmera rīngī

English: The sucker pseudostem should be cut off near its corm and the sharp point of the knife twisted into the growing point to kill off the sucker permanently.
Kikuyu: Mūkori wa thuna ūbatīe kūrengwo wega na mwena ūria wina mūthia kūgarūruo nīguo ndūkamere rīngi.

English: During this operation, care must be taken not to harm other daughter plants.
Kikuyu: Ukīruta wīra ūyū tigīrīra nīweka na ūbacīrīru nīgūo ndugathūkie mūmera ucio ungī.

English: In the course of time, the banana plants tend to grow away from the original space whereby the gaps between the plants become smaller.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa kahinda, thuna cia marigū ciambagīrīria kuehera gītīna-īnī nīūndū nī hatumanaga.

English: At this point, it is necessary to remove the plants that stand close to each other.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīrī he bata kūeheria mīmera īrīa īrugamīte hakuhī

English: If the original pattern of the banana plantation becomes completely distorted, then the plantation should be cleared and newly planted.
Kikuyu: marigū mangīcenjia mūbango wa ūrīa mabangītwo mabatīe kweherio na kūhandūo mangī.

English: What is Deleafing?
Kikuyu: Deleafing nī kuuga atīa?

English: Old leaves and sheaths are susceptible to infections and can host infections if not removed in time.
Kikuyu: Mathangū makūrū nīmanyitagwo nī mīrimū mangīa kūeherio ihinda-inī rīrīa rīagīrīire.

English: Removal of old leaves helps in management of the Sigatoka leaf spots, limiting its spread to young leaves and plants, while the removal of old sheaths eliminates hiding places for adult banana weevils.
Kikuyu: Kweheria mathangū makūrū nīgūteithagia kūnyihia mūrimū wa Sigatoka na gūtamba kūrī mathangū marīa manini kana mīmera ingī, kūrūta mīraba mīkūru nīgūteithagia kweheria kūrīa mboca cia marīgu cīngīhitha.

English: In addition, old leaves that hang downwards shield the young plants from sunlight.
Kikuyu: Ningī, mathangū makūrū marīa macuhaga nathī nī magitagīra mīmera mīnini kuumana na rīua

English: It is therefore recommended to remove all old leaves and sheaths that have attained natural senescence and use them as mulch,
Kikuyu: Koguo nī harī bata kūruta mahuti mothe makūrū na ūmahūthīre kwarīra ta mūcing'i.

English: . It is, however, important that enough leaves are left on the plant to produce a good quality bunch.
Kikuyu: He bata gūtīgīrīra mūmera waku wīna mahuti ma kūigana nīguo ūciare wega

English: The average number of leaves per banana plant should be 8 to 10 leaves at flowering and at harvest.
Kikuyu: O mūtī wa marigū nī ūbatīe gūkoruo na mahuti 8-10 ūkīruta kīro na ūkīgetha

English: Complete deleafing of the plant prior to harvesting is not recommended as this starts the ripening process, before the plant is actually ready
Kikuyu: Kūruta mahuti mothe mbere ya kūgetha ti wega tondū nī gūtumaga marigū meruhe na ihenya mūmera ūtagimarīte wega.

English: Correct way to weed bananas?
Kikuyu: Njīra njega ya kūrīmīra marigū nī īrīku?

English: Care should be taken to avoid any disturbance of the roots and the developing suckers.
Kikuyu: ūmenyereri nī ūbatīe nīgūo kūgīrīrīria gūthūkia mīrī na thuna iria irakūra

English: Earthing up of the stem base is required in windy areas.
Kikuyu: Ikīria itina tīīri wega kundū kūrīa kūrī rūhuho rūnene.

English: What is the importance of pruning and propping
Kikuyu: Bata wa gūceha nīkīi?

English: To provide bigger and higher quality bunches, bananas have to be de-suckered regularly to control any unwanted sucker growth.
Kikuyu: Nīgūtūmaga gītina gīkorwo na hinya na marigū ma gīkīro kia igūrū, marigū nī mabatīe kūrūtuo thuna iria itarabatarania.

English: Only three pseudo-stems should be allowed to remain on each corm: one bearing one half grown, and one just sprouting.
Kikuyu: Mīkori ītatū no yo ībatiī gūtiguo īkūre. Ūmwe wa gūciara, umwe ūkūrīte nuthu na ūmwe ūgīthundūka

English: Surplus suckers should be removed as early as possible in their development and perhaps used as planting material.
Kikuyu: thuna nyingī ībatiī kweherio o ciathundūka na rīngī ūhūthīke kūhanda kūndū kūngī.

English: Dead leaves should be removed at least twice a year.
Kikuyu: Mahuti momū mabatīe kūrutuo maita matanyīhīīre meri o mwaka

English: After harvesting, the pseudo-stem should be cut off from the plant at ground level.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa kūgetha rī, mīramba ībatiī kūrengūo harīa gītina-inī

English: Propping of the fruit bearing pseudo-stem should be done to prevent breakage caused by heavy bunches.
Kikuyu: Tira mūramba ūria ūraciara nīguo ndūkagūe kana kuunīka

English: Done with wood or bamboo, for bamboo requires digging a hole about 40 to 60 cm deep at the base of the stem to install the prop.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra mūtī kana mūrangi, mūrangi nī ūbataraga kwenjeruo ta 40cm-60cm nathīī nīguo kūnyitīīrīra mūrangi ūcio

English: Tie the bunch to the prop near the portion where the fruit stalk emerges from the stem.
Kikuyu: Tira mūramba harīa marigū marathundūkīra

English: Y-sticks can also be used for staking.
Kikuyu: Mītī īhana Y no īhūthīke nīguo kūnyitīīrīra wega

English: This helps avoid unnecessary logging of the stems especially due to massive fruiting
Kikuyu: Gwīka ūū nī kūgiragīrīria kūgia na marigū maaīngī mangīrehe nduma

English: What crops can be intercropped with banana?
Kikuyu: Nī mīmera īrīkū īngīhanduo hamwe na marigū?

English: Perennial crops such as coffee can be intercropped with bananas for the provision of shade especially at establishment.
Kikuyu: Mīmera ya īmera nene ta kahūa no īhandūo hamwe na marigū nīgūo kūhe kīruru

English: They also benefit the banana by recycling nutrients from deep capture, because of their rooting system, via litter fall.
Kikuyu: Nīteithagia marigū na kūhee īrio iria njega nīundu wa mīri yao.

English: Bean is the annual crop most commonly associated with banana.
Kikuyu: Mboco nīo mūmera wa omwaka ūrīa ūnyitithanagio na marigū.

English: The two crops are compatible in a multi-story system as bean does not compete with banana above ground and is more shade tolerant than most other food crops.
Kikuyu: Mīmera īno īrī nīīhotaga gukūra hamwe na mboco ndītoragio nīkīruru kīa marigū kūringithanio na mīmera īrīa īngī

English: Maize and sweet potatoes are intercropped with the young banana plants and are phased out at canopy closure.
Kikuyu: Mbembe na ngwachī nī ihandanagīrio hamwe na marigū nīguo kūgīa kīruru

English: Fruit trees like jack fruit and papaw are established with the plantation as sources of fruit.
Kikuyu: Matunda ta Jack fruit na mababaī mahandagūo marigūinī magatūhe matunda

English: Is it essential for a banana field to have trenches?
Kikuyu: He bata marigū kūenjerūo mītaro?

English: No, it is not essential.
Kikuyu: Aca, hatirī bata

English: Trenches are usually dug where water flows through the banana field, in order to trap the water and also control erosion.
Kikuyu: Mītaro yenjagūo kūrīa maaī maraikūruka nīgūo gūtega maaī na kūgīrīrīria tīīrī kūra

English: When is the best time to dig trenches? Can one dig trenches before planting?
Kikuyu: Nī īhīnda rīrīkū rīega rīa kwenja mītaro

English: Trenches are ideally dug immediately after the banana field is established, so that the young plants can benefit from the water that they hold.
Kikuyu: Mītaro yagīrīiruo nī kwenjuo harīa marigū mahandūo nīguo mīmera īrīa mīnini īteithīke nī maaī mau

English: It is ok to dig trenches before planting, but it will be best if you dig them after digging the planting holes.
Kikuyu: He bata kwenja mītaro mbere ya kūhanda, no he bata makīria kwenja warīkia kwenja marima ma kūhanda

English: How would you control monkeys that destroy the bananas?
Kikuyu: īngīgirīrīria atia therū iria ithūkagia mūgūnda wa marigū?

English: You can use a scarecrow in the banana field, or have the field guarded by a dog to scare away the monkeys.
Kikuyu: No ūthondeke kamūndū mūgūnda-inī kana ūige ngui nīguo ciingate therū icio.

English: Why is ‘matooke’ from Western Uganda different in taste to that from Central Uganda?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī marigū ma Rūgūrū rwa Uganda marī mūcamo ngūrani na ma Uganda ya gatagatī?

English: This is caused by a number of factors, such as the soils, the rainfall pattern, management practices, etc.
Kikuyu: Mūcamo ūyu ngūrani ūcūngagīrīrio nī maūndū matiganīte ta, mūthemba wa tīri, ūria mbura īraura na ūrīa mūgūnda ūrarorūo

English: Can I grow bananas together with pineapples?
Kikuyu: No nīme marigū hamwe na mananathi?

English:
Yes, bananas can be grown together with pineapples, but if bananas are the main crop, then
they need to be planted using a relatively bigger spacing.
Kikuyu: Nomarīmwe hamwe mananathi na marigū no angīkoruo marigū nīmo ūtanyate kūrīma mūno tīgīrīra niwatigithūkania wega marima

English: When is the best time to cut off the male bud?
Kikuyu: Nī ihinda rīrīkū rīega rīa kūrenga makūmba ma njamba?

English: When all the fingers have developed from the flowers.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa njara ciothe ciakūra kuma kīro-inī

English: How long does a banana take to mature?
Kikuyu: marigū makūraga thutha wa kahinda kaigana atīa?

English: Bananas take about one year from planting before the fruits are ready to eat.
Kikuyu: marigū makūraga thutha wa mwaka ūmwe kuma ihinda rīa kūhanda matarītuo

English: Is it true that hoes should not be used to control weeds in bananas?
Kikuyu: Nīma atī dībatiī kūhūthīra theci ngīrīma marigū?

English: It not true.
Kikuyu: Ti ma

English: However, precaution should be taken when using a hoe to not damage the roots of the bananas.
Kikuyu: No he bata kūmenyerera mīri ya marigū ūkīhūthīra theci

English: Is it okay to spray herbicides in a banana field intercropped with coffee?
Kikuyu: Nī wega kūhuhīra dawa cīa ria mūgūnda inī wa marigū hamwe na kahūa?

English: Yes, it is okay as long as the herbicide is used according to the manufacturer’s specifications.
Kikuyu: Hatirī thīna angīkorūo dawa īyo nīyahūthīrūo kūringana na ūrīa yedekithītio kuma kūrī athondeki

English: What tools are used to harvest bananas?
Kikuyu: Nī indo īrīkū cibatie kūgetha marigū?

English: On banana plantations, a wide variety of tools are used in order to harvest and maintain crops.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda-īni wa marigū nī kūrī indo nyīngī cīngīhūthīruo

English: A few tools that are consistently used on banana plantations are shearing knives, pruners, and hand saws.
Kikuyu: Hamwe ha indo icio nī, tūhīū twa gūceha, magathī na tho cia moko

English: What happens if you pick bananas too early?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī kīngīhanīka ūngīgetha marigū matarī  meru?

English: They must have sufficiently developed their shape in diameter and length.
Kikuyu: Nīmabatīe gūkūra wega harī ūraihu na warīe

English: If harvested too early, the taste will not develop well.
Kikuyu: Magethagwo tene, matirī mūcamo mwega

English: What are the best practices for fertilizing banana plants?
Kikuyu: Nī atīa wega gūīkīra bataraitha harī marigū?

English: Conduct soil tests to determine nutrient deficiencies and apply a balanced fertilizer regimen based on the results.
Kikuyu: Thima tīri nīgūo ūmenye nī bataraitha īrīkū ībataire kūrīngana na maumīrīra ma ūthuthuria

English: What is the best fertilizer for bananas in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī bataraitha īrīkū njega ībatiī gūīkīruo marigū-inī Kenya?

English: At planting, about 200 g of triple super phosphate (TSP) should be applied per plant.
Kikuyu: ūkīhanda īkīra gramu 200 cia triple super phosphate (TSP) o harī mūmera

English: An early and good supply of nitrogen fertilizer is essential to accelerate the growth of pseudo-stems and faster flowering.
Kikuyu: ūhei wa bataraitha ya Nitrogen kabere nī ūteithagia gūkūra kwa naihenya kwa mūmera na gūtūma mīramba īkorūo na hinya

English: What is the best manure for bananas?
Kikuyu: Nī thumu ūrīku mwega wa marigū

English: Organic Grow - If you prefer to start your banana tree off with a high-nitrogen feed then Organic Grow is ideal with an NPK ratio of 7-2-3.
Kikuyu: Organic Grow- ūngītua kwambīrīria mūmera waku na nitrogen nyingī, ūhe Organic Grow īrīa īri na NPK gīthimo kīa 7-2-3

English: Organic Bloom - With an NPK ratio of 6-5-6, Organic Bloom will ensure your banana plants has everything it needs throughout the growing season.
Kikuyu: Organic Bloom- īrī na gīthimo kīa 6-5-6, Organic bloom ītigagīrīra marigū maku mena irio iria ībatiī kīmera gīothe

English: What is the recommended fertilizer and Manure to apply banana?
Kikuyu: Nī bataraitha na thumu ūrīkū wendekithītio?

English: At planting, about 200 g of triple super phosphate (TSP) should be applied per plant.
Kikuyu: ūkīhanda rī, hūthīra ta gramu 200 cia Super phosphate TSP mūmera inī.

English: An early and good supply of nitrogen fertilizer is essential to accelerate the growth of pseudo-stems and faster flowering.
Kikuyu: ūheo wa tene na wanaihenya wa bataraitha ya Nitrogen wī bata harī kūhiūhia ūkūria wa narua wa mūmera

English: To 300 g of calcium ammonium nitrate (CAN) should be distributed around each stool per year, together with 125 g of TSP, which is worked into the soil.
Kikuyu: Gramu 300 cia Calcium Ammonium Nitrate CAN ūbatie gwītirīrio gītina-inī harī o mwaka hamwe na gramu 125 cia TSP nīguo kūruta wīra tīīrī- inī

English: Two to four debes of decomposed farmyard manure is applied per stem per year before the rains.
Kikuyu: Debe 2- 4 cia thumu nīciheagūo mūmera harī o mwaka mbura īta naura

English: This is applied on the outer diameter of the canopy.
Kikuyu: īka ūū haraihu na gītina-inī

English: A short forked hoe is used to incorporate the manure shallowly and carefully, to ensure no root damage.
Kikuyu: Hūma nini īteithagia gūtukania thumu wega na tīri na kūgitīra gūthūkīa mīri

English: Does mulching control banana weevils?
Kikuyu: Hihi kwarīra marigū nī kūgiragīrīria gūtamba kwa mboca cia marīgu?

English: No, mulching does not control banana weevils.
Kikuyu: Aca, Kwarīra marigū gūtigiragīrīria gūtamba kwa mboca cia marīgu

English: In fact, it increases their multiplication since it provides them with a favourable environment to reproduce.
Kikuyu: Makīrīa, nī kuongerera ūciarithanio wacio tondū nīutethagia gucīhe handū hega ha gūciarana

English: This is why it is not recommended to apply mulch close to the banana plant.
Kikuyu: Nīkīo tī wega kwarīra hakuhī na mīmera ya marigū

English: Can a farmer who mulches his plantation but does not apply fertilizers obtain big bunches?
Kikuyu: Hihi mūrīmi waragira mīmera yake mūcing'i na ndaheaga bataraitha no agīe honge nene?

English: A farmer could still get quite big bunches, but they would be bigger if fertilizer was also used.
Kikuyu: No agīe honge nene, no agīe nene makīria angīhūthīra bataraitha

English: The best bunches are produced where all the recommended agronomic practices are carried out, including both mulching and applying fertilizer.
Kikuyu: Honge njega cigīagūo ūngīrūmīrīra mendekithia mothe ta kwarīra mīmera yaku hamwe na kuhe bataraitha

English: As mulching conserves water in the soil, is it also necessary to dig trenches in a mulched banana
Kikuyu: Kwarīra marigū gūteithagia kūnyihia kūra kwa maaī, harī bata kwenja mītaro harīa warīīre

English: If there is water flowing through the field, the trenches are needed even if the field is mulched.
Kikuyu: Okorūo Harī maaī  maragera mūgūnda inī, mītaro nī ībatiī onangīkorūo niwarīire mūgūnda waku

English: Mulching and trenches have different roles:
Kikuyu: Kwarīra na kwenja mītaro nīrī mawira ngūrani

English: mulching conserves water in the soil, whereas the trenches trap the water within the field and also stop topsoil from being eroded.
Kikuyu: Kwarīra kūgitagīrīria maaī marīa marī tīri-inī , mītaro nayo ītegaga maaī marīa marī mūgūnda na ougūo kūgītīra tīri wa igūrū

English: Which is better for banana production: chicken or cow manure?
Kikuyu: Nī thumu ūrīku mwega mūgūnda īnī wa marigū?

English: Both types of manure are good, and the type of manure you use depends on availability.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba yothe ya thumu nī mīega na ūkīhūthīra o mūthemba nī kūraringana na woneku waguo

English: However, chicken manure is used up faster than cow manure, so cow manure is better from that point of view.
Kikuyu: Harī o ugūo rī, thumu wa ngūkū nī ūhūthīkaga narua makīria gukīra wa ng'ombe, koguo wa ng'ombe nī mwega makīria

English: How can I tell fake fertilizers from genuine fertilizers?
Kikuyu: ingī kūrana atīa bataraitha njega harī njūrū

English: The only way is to buy fertilizers from a reputable agro-input dealer or company.
Kikuyu: Njīra njega no kūgūra bataraitha yaku harī mwendia ūīkaine weega kuma kambūni njega?

English: What is the maximum amount of organic fertilizers recommended for a banana plant?
Kikuyu: Nī gīkīro kīrīkū batie kūhe bataraitha ya kīmerera harī mūmera wa marigū?

English: Is it possible to ‘overdose’ with organic fertilizers, as is the case with inorganic ones?
Kikuyu: No kūhoteke atī nīndakīria gīthimo kīa bataraitha ya kīmerera?

English: No, overdose with organic fertilizers is very unlikely, but farmers should avoid using too much as it is a waste.
Kikuyu: Aca, gūkīria gīthimo nī hinya. Harī o ugūo arīmī nī mabatīe gūīthema gūīkīra makīria nīguo kūgirīrīria gūthūkangia

English: For bananas, apply only two basins (~ 20 kg) of organic manure at the beginning of the rains.
Kikuyu: Harī marigū, īkīra irai igirī (kilo 20) cia thumu mbura īkīambia

English: Is there any relationship between nutrient deficiency and diseases in bananas?
Kikuyu: Nī harī ūndū ūnyitithanagia wagi wa irio na mīrīmū harī marigū?

English: Yes, there is a big relationship.
Kikuyu: īī nī harī

English: Bananas that lack some nutrients are stressed and are much more easily attacked by diseases.
Kikuyu: marigū marīa magaga mūthemba wa irio nīmagūmagīrūo nī mīrīmū mūno

English: Can nutrient deficiency weaken the bananas?
Kikuyu: Hihi, kwaga irio harī mūmera nogūtūme mūmera kwaga hinya?

English: Yes, nutrient deficiency weakens the bananas and also leads to poor harvest.
Kikuyu: īī nigūo, wagi wa irio no gūtūme mūmera wage hinya na mwisho wage maciaro mega

English: How do I market my bananas in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Ngwetha atīa thoko ya marigū makwa Kenya?

English: You can sell bananas in local markets, to fruit vendors, or export them.
Kikuyu: No wendie marigū kūrī thoko nini, endia a matunda kana nja ya būrūri

English: Building relationships with buyers and cooperatives can help access markets.
Kikuyu: Gūthondeka ūnyitanīri wa agūri na honge cia ūgūri nī iteithagia kūona thoko.

English: How can I control banana weevils on my farm?
Kikuyu: īngīnyihia atīa mboca cia marīgu mūgūnda-inī wakwa?

English: Control measures include using healthy planting materials, proper sanitation, and applying appropriate pesticides when necessary.
Kikuyu: Njīra cia kugīrīrīria nī hamwe na, kūhūthīra Indo therū na kūhuthīra dawa cia tūtambi īria ibatie rīrīa kūbatiī.

English: What is Panama disease, and how can it be managed?
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Panama nī ūrīkū na ūngīrorūo atīa?

English: Panama disease is a devastating fungal disease.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa Panama nī mūrimū mūru wa marigū

English: Management includes planting resistant varieties and maintaining strict sanitation practices.
Kikuyu: Gūthaitīra nī hamwe na kuhanda mbeu iria hetūkie na cīngīītiria mīrīmū hamwe na kūrora ūtheru wa mūgūnda

English: How do I prevent diseases like Black Sigatoka in my banana plantation?
Kikuyu: īngīgitia atīa mūrimū ta Black Sigatoka mūgūnda inī wakwa wa Marigū

English: Implementing proper disease management practices, including regular fungicide applications and sanitation, can help prevent Black Sigatoka.
Kikuyu: Kūmenyerera mūgūnda nī hamwe kūhūra dawa cia fungicide na gūtigīrīra kūrī na ūtheru wa mūgūnda. gwīka ūū nī gūkūgirīrīria Black Sigatoka

English: How can I improve my banana plantation?
Kikuyu: ingīagīria atīa makīria mūgūnda wakwa wa Marigū?

English: For a fine plantation, you must:,keep the soil clean;
Kikuyu: Harī mūgunda mwega, tīgīrīra tīri nī mūtheru

English: apply fertilizers;
Kikuyu: Gītira mūmera kumana na ndwari na tūtambi

English: prune the plants;
Kikuyu: Rūnga mīmera yaku

English: prevent the plants from falling;
Kikuyu: Gitīra mūmera dūkagūe

English: look after the fruit;
Kikuyu: Rora matunda

English: How do you control pests in banana plantation?
Kikuyu: ūgiragīrīria atīa tūtambi na mīrīmū mūgūnda inī wa marigū?

English:
Chemical Control
Spray soapy water or insecticidal soap on plants thoroughly on petioles, furled leaves, whorls or on young suckers.
Kikuyu: Dawa cīa kūhuthīra-Huhīra maaī ma thabuni kana thabuni wa tūtambi harī mīmera wega harī petiols, mathangū makūnjīkanu, whorls kana kūrī thuna iria nini

English: Spray Dimethoate (75ml/100lit) or Diazinon (1.5ml/lit) or Acephate (1.3g/lit) on infested plants and suckers.
Kikuyu: Huhīra Dimethoate ( 75ml/100ltr) kana Diazinon( 1.5ml/ltr) kana Acephate ( 1.3g/ ltr) harī mīmera kana thuna iria ng'umīre.

English: Spray Methyl Demeton 25 EC 0.05%
Kikuyu: Huhīra Methyl Demeton 25EC 0.05%

English: What is a major problem with banana plantations?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī thīna makīria harī mīgūnda ya marigū?

English: The major environmental problems arise from the production of bananas for export or from large-scale commercial production of bananas for local markets.
Kikuyu: Thīna mūnene harī ūkūria wa marigū maingī na ma naja wa būrūri kana gūkūria nīūndū wa thoko cia o gūkū būrūri-inī

English: These include habitat conversion, soil erosion and degradation, pollution from agrochemical use, solid waste, and water usage.
Kikuyu: Nī hamwe na gūcejia harīa makūragio, tīri kwenyūka na gūthūka, gūtukanīka na dawa iria ihuhagīrio, guteo gwa takataka na maaī

English: What is a Banana weevil (Cosmopolites sordidus)
Kikuyu: Mbocha cia marigū nīkīī (Cosmopolites Sordidus)

English: The Banana weevil is a very damaging banana root borer.
Kikuyu: Mboca cia marīgu nī ithūkagia na gūtūra marima mīri-inī

English: The larvae bore into corms, suckers, and roots and lead to extensive root destruction.
Kikuyu: Kīgunyū nīkīo gītūraga mīkori, na mīri na īgatūma mīri īthūke.

English: This leads to stunted plant growth and eventually premature toppling of the plants and plant death.
Kikuyu: Nī ītumaga marigū mekururie gūkūra na mwisho makambia kūgwa na gūkua mūmera wothe

English: How to manage Banana weevil (Cosmopolites sordidus)
Kikuyu: ūngīgirīrīria atīa gūtamba kwa mboca cia marīgū

English: Recommendations to farmers for management of the banana weevil:
Kikuyu: Wendekīthia kūrī arīmī nī hamwe na:

English: > Use clean planting materials - As mentioned earlier, use tissue culture plantlets or treated suckers for planting new banana plantations.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra Indo therū ūkīhanda - Handa mīmera ītarī na mīrīmū.

English: > Crop rotation - In heavily infested gardens, it is highly recommended to progressively destroy the entire garden and remove all banana plants and their corms.
Kikuyu: > Garūra mīmera - He bata kūmunya marigū mothe mīgūnda-inī īrīa mīgūmīre nī tūtambi n mīrimū na ūhande mīmera mūthemba ūngī.

English: Chop them to dry or compost them and plant other crops in the field for 1 to 2 years.
Kikuyu: Tinangia mīramba iyo nīguo ithondeke thumu na ūhande mīmera īngī harī mwaka ūmwe nginya īrī

English: Make sure that all corms and roots are destroyed during that time.
Kikuyu: Tīgīrīra mbeū na mīrI yothe nī yathūkio

English: > Ensure field hygiene - It is a common practice to split the pseudostem after every harvest.
Kikuyu: ūtheru wa mūgūnda- he bata kūrenga mūramba o wagetha

English: The stem is split open and the sheaths are spread out to dry so that weevil eggs and larvae are destroyed.
Kikuyu: Muramba ūrengaguo nīguo matūmbī ma mboca macinūo na mathūke

English: The sheaths should, however, be laid about 2 feet away from the banana stool, like any other mulching material in a banana plantation.
Kikuyu: Mīramba ībatiī kūigūo fiti īgīrī haraihu na gītina kīa marigū ota ūrīa gwīkagūo harī kwarīra mīmera īrīa īngī.

English: Do not move banana residue material (pseudostems, corms, sheaths) from one field to another in order to limit transmission of weevils.
Kikuyu: Matigio ma marigū matibatie kūrutūo mūgūnda inī nginya  ūngī nīgūo kūgirīrīria gūtamba kwa mboca

English: > Trap the weevils - Laying traps to catch banana weevils and killing the weevils collected from traps can be an effective method of controlling weevils especially in small gardens.
Kikuyu: Tega mboca- Iga mītego yaku nīguo kūnyitia mboca cia marīgū na gūciūraga nīguo kūnyihia gūtherema harī mīgūnda mīnini.

English: The weevils are mobile at night and can be trapped by baiting the field with slices of banana pseudostems.
Kikuyu: Mboca nī ithiaga mūno ūtukū koguo no cīarīrwo mītego harī marigū matinangie weega.

English: Traps should be cleared every 3 days so that they do not become a breeding ground for the weevils.
Kikuyu: mītego īyo ībatiī kweherio thutha wa thīkū ithatū nīguo kūgirīrīria gūcīarana makīrīa.

English: All trapped weevils should be picked from the baits and destroyed or fed to poultry.
Kikuyu: Mboca iria cianyitūo cibatīe kweherio mītego-inī kana iheo ngūkū kana nyoni.

English: > Keep the banana stool clean - Mulching materials or any debris should not be put close or within the stool in order to deny weevils a hiding place.
Kikuyu: Tigīrīra gītinainī kīa marigū nī gītheru, arīra haraihu na gītīna nīgūo kūgiria mboca gūcīarana

English: Also, any banana plant remains from infected gardens should not be used as mulch in clean banana gardens.
Kikuyu: Oūgūo rī, matigari mūmera wa marigū o wothe harī mūgūnda mūrwaru ndūbatie kūhūthīrūo kwarīra mīmera ya marigū harī mūgunda ūngī

English: > Use natural pesticides - Such as wood ash, tephrosia leaf dust, chilli preparations with animal urine, tithonia leaf extract, and neem oil.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra dawa cīa tūtambī cia kīmerera - ta muhu, rūkūngū rwa tephrosia, biribiri, mathugumo ma mahiū, mahuti ma tithonia na maguta ma neem.

English: These materials should be applied at the base of the plants around the stool and around infected pseudostems.
Kikuyu: Indo ici cibatie gūīkīrūo gītinainī kīa mūmera mwena-inī ūrīa mūgūmīre

English: However, it is important for certified organic farmers to check with their certification body before using any factory-made products for weevil control.
Kikuyu: Harī oūguo rī, he bata harī arīmi a kīmerera kūrora indo cīumīte igandainī nīguo kūgirīrīria gūtamba kwa mboca

English: What are Nematodes
Kikuyu: Mīnyongoro nī kī?

English: • Nematodes are the most damaging pests causing over 70 % loss of the crop Damages:
Kikuyu: Mīnyongoro nīyo īthūkagia marigū makīrīa harī gīcunjī kīa 70%

English: • Lesions and tunnels within the rhizome
Kikuyu: Lesion na tunnel thīinī wa rhizome

English: • Root destruction leads to toppling of mature plant, especially when the wind blows or during bearing stage
Kikuyu: Gūthūūka kwa mīri nī gūtūmaga mūmera ūgūe makīria rīrīa kūrī rūhuho kana rīrīa ūraciara

English: How to manage Nematodes
Kikuyu: mīnyoo ya marigū īhūranagūo nayo atīa

English: Recommendations to farmers for management of the banana nematodes:
Kikuyu: medekithia kūrī arīmi ma njīra cia kūhūrana na mīnyoo ya marigū

English: > Use clean planting materials - Use tissue materials or treat banana suckers to ensure that no infections are introduced into the new fields.
Kikuyu: kūhūthīra  mbeū theru- kūhūthīra mbeū cia mūciarithanio kana gūtheria thuuna nīguo kūnina mīrimū.

English: > Crop rotation - In heavily infested plantations, it is highly recommended to destroy the garden and remove all banana plants and their corms.
Kikuyu: kūgarūrīra mìmera- mīgūnda ya marigū īrīa īna mīnyoo yendekithītio ķūmunya marigū moothe na thuuna

English:
Chop them to dry or compost them. Nematodes will survive in the soil for about one year
if deprived of host materials.
Kikuyu: kūmatinangia na kūmomithia kana kūmatua thumu. mīnyoo īngīaga gīikaro īngītūra ta mwaka tu

English: Therefore, after uprooting all banana material from the infected plot; grow other crops for a period for 1.5 to 2 years, followed by recultivation with clean banana planting materials.
Kikuyu: kūhanda mīmera īngī ta mwaka na nuthu nginya īīrī thuutha wa kūmunya marigū kūrīa kwīna mīnyoo kūrūmagīrīrwo nī kūhanda marigū weerū

English: > Increase soil fertility - Addition of compost in planting holes, top-dressing with organic manures and mulching with organic materials increases soil life activity and has a negative effect on soil pests like nematodes.
Kikuyu: kwongerera ūnoru wa tīīri-kwongerera thumu marima ma kūhanda na kwarīra mahuti nīgūthondekaga tīīri na kūhotomia mīnyoo.

English: It encourages how to manage nematode and weevil damage
Kikuyu: nīīteithagīrīria kūnyihia wanangi wa mīnyoo na mbūca

English: > Field hygiene - Besides planting materials, nematodes can also be spread through soil carried on farm tools, farmers shoes/feet and banana residues, mainly corms.
Kikuyu: gūtheria mūgūnda-tiga tu kuumana na thuuna rī, mīnyoo nīītambagio na tīīri kuuma na indo cia kūrīma,iiratū cia magūrū ,mīramba na macoya

English: Therefore, ensure that proper cleaning is done of all tools and farmers shoes/feet before entering a healthy garden.
Kikuyu: Tīgīrīra ūtheru mwega wīkuo kūrī indo cia wīra hamwe na iratū na magūrū ma mūrīmi atanaingīra mugunda mwerū

English: Banana corms should not be shared between gardens to limit the spread of nematode infections.
Kikuyu: Ndugaciarithanie mīgūnda ngūrani nīguo kūgirīrīria gūtamba kwa mūrimū wa mīnyongoro.

English:
Control:
• Use of clean planting material (Tissue Culture or by hot water treatment)
Kikuyu: Hūthīra indo theru cia kūhanda ( indo cia mūciarithanio kana maaī mahiū)

English: • Use “Tithonia” and “Mexican Marigold” in banana farm as green manure
Kikuyu: Hūthira Tithonia kana Mexican Marigold harī mūgūnda wa marigū ta thumu

English: • Apply farmyard manure or poultry manure
Kikuyu: Hūthīra thumu wa mahiū kana wa ngūkū

English: • Use of nematicides such as Azadirachtin (Achook®), Ethoprophos (MOCAP GR 10®)
Kikuyu: Hūthīra Nematicides ta Azadirachtin( Achook) Ethroprophos(MOCAP GR10)

English: How to identify and manage banana thrips
Kikuyu: ūngimenya atīa na kūgitīra tūtambi twa Thrips Harī marigū

English:
Banana Thrips
Damages:
• Silvery patches on the fruits that later turn brown
Kikuyu:
Thrips cia marigū
Gūthūkia
maroro marahenia harī matunda marīa macenjagia thutha-inī makahana rangi wa gītīri

English:
• The skin of heavily infested fruit may crack permitting secondary infection which results in fruit rot making it unattractive hence lowering its
marketability
Control
• Removal of male flowers
Kikuyu:
Ngothi ya matunda marīa magūmīirūo maambagia gwatūka na kūreka magwatio nī mūrimū wa kerī. Matunda mambagia kūbutha na kūaga kūgucīrīria na macūngīrīro nī thoko kunyiha
Kūgītīra: Eheria kīro kia njamba

English: • Covering of bunches/bagging
Kikuyu: Kunīka ngoro cia marigū na njīra ya gūciharūkia iratathi.

English:
• Use of insecticides, such as
– Deltamethrin (Decis 2.5 EC®)
– Pirimiphos-Methyl (ACTELLIC
25EC®)
Kikuyu:
Hūthīra dawa cia tūtambi ta
- Deltamethrin( Decis 2.5 EC)
- Pirimiphos- methyl ( Actelic 25 EC)

English: How to identify and manage Banana Weevil Bore
Kikuyu: ūngīmenya atīa na kūgitīra mboca

English:
Banana Weevil Borer
Identification:
• Weevil: A brown-black weevil with a curved hard shell and rotting
Kikuyu:
Mboca cia marīgū
Mboca njirū na ya gītīīri na īna gakūmbi komū

English: • Borer (grub) form irregular tunnels in the rhizome reducing it to a mass of rotten tissue
Kikuyu: (Grub) Yenjaga marima matarī manyoroku na ūguo īkabuthia

English:
Damages:
• The leaves of infested plants turn yellow, wither and die prematurely
Kikuyu: Mahuti magūmīre macenjagia rangi wa ngoikoni, makahoha na magakua biū

English: • Infested plants are easily blown over the ground by wind
Kikuyu: mīmera īrīa īgūmīiruo nī raithī kūgūa thī nīundu wa rūhuho

English:
Control:
• Use clean planting material for propagation (Tissue Culture or hot water treatment)
Kikuyu: Kūgītīra- Huthira indo theru cia kūhanda ( gūciarithania)

English: • Do NOT leave suckers and other planting materials overnight in the field, as the weevils may lay eggs on them
Kikuyu: Dūgatige thuna na mīmera īngī mirenge tondū mboca nocirekerie matūmbī mahuti-inī

English: • After harvest, cut stems at the ground level and cover the cut surface with a layer of earth to prevent entry of the weevil
Kikuyu: Wagetha rī, renga mīramba harīa thī biū  na ūhake tīīrī nīguo mboca ītīkaingīre

English: • Cut harvested stems into very small pieces to allow faster drying
Kikuyu: Tinangia mīramba rīa mīgethe tūcunjī tūcunjī nīguo tūme narua

English: How to identify and manage panama disease
Kikuyu: ūngīmenya atīa na kūgitīra Mūrimū wa Panama

English: Panama Disease
Kikuyu: mūrimū wa Panama

English: General Descriptions:
Kikuyu: Mītugo yaguo

English: • A soil-borne disease also known as “Fusarium Wilt”
Kikuyu: Nī mūrimū wa tīri ūrīa wītaguo Fusarium wilt

English: • Fungus attacks roots and blocks vascular system causing wilts Symptoms:
Kikuyu: Fungus ici cigūmagīra mīri na gūtūma mūmera ūhohe

English: • The older leaves turn yellow and collapse while still green at the base
Kikuyu: Mahuti makūrū macenjagia rangi ūgatuika ngoikoni na makagūa gītīna inī kīrī na rangi wa grini

English: • The leaves fall in order, from the oldest to the youngest until they hang around the pseudo-stem like a skirt, and dry up
Kikuyu: Mahuti magūaga mabuatanīte, kuuma rīrīa rīkūrū nginya rīrīa rī nini magathiūngūkīria mūramba ta thikati na thutha-inī makoma

English: • The emerging heart leaf may die while the stem remains erect till it decays and falls over
Kikuyu: Ihuti rīrīa rīrathundūka no rīkue na mūramba ūgatigūo ūrūgamīte, ūkabutha na mwishoini ūkagua.

English: • Diseased plants fail to produce normal fruit and die before the fruit stalk is fully developed
Kikuyu: mūmera mūrūaru nī ūremagūo gūciara matunda mega na makuaga itunda ritaithondokete wega

English:
Control:
• Apple and Gross Michel/Kampala are highly susceptible to this disease
Kikuyu: Kūgītīra:Apple na Gross Michel/ Kampala nīciītagīria mūrimū ūyū

English: • Use of resistant varieties, such as Giant Cavendish, Lacatan or FHIA hybrids
Kikuyu: Hūthīra mīthemba īrīa īngītiria ta Giant Cavendish, Lacatan or FHIA hybrids

English: • Observe quarantine
Kikuyu: Tigithūkania

English: • Use disease-free materials (Tissue Culture and clean suckers)
Kikuyu: Hūthīra thuna na gūciarithania na mīmera ītari mīrīmū

English: How to identify and manage black leaf streak
Kikuyu: ūngemenya/kūgītira atīa black leaf streak

English: Black Sigatoka/ Black Leaf Streak
Kikuyu: Black Sigatoka/ Black Leaf Streak

English:
General Descriptions:
• A fungal disease that destroys banana leaves also called Leaf Streak Sigatoka
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa fungal- ūthūkagia mahuti ma marigū, ūgetūo Leaf Streak Sigatoka

English: • It can seriously reduce crop yield
Kikuyu: īnyihagia magetha na gīkīro kīnene

English:
Symptoms:
• First symptoms are narrow, rusty, reddish-brown streaks on the underside of leaves
Kikuyu: Dariri- mahuti gucekeha , magacu, magatunīhīra thī wa mahuti

English: • These become dark brown or black spots on both surfaces and develop yellow margins & grey centres
Kikuyu: Thutha ūcio makaga maroro ma rangi wa gītīīri miena īrī na mīthia kūgīa rangi wa ngoikoni.

English: • It causes significant reduction in leaf area, premature ripening and yield loss of up to 50 % or more
Kikuyu: īnyihagia mahuti, matunda makeruha matakinyanīīre na maciaro kūnyiha na gīcunjī kīa 50 harī igana kana makīrīa

English:
Control:
• Cultural practices such as removal of affected leaves, adequate spacing of plants and efficient drainage within orchards
Kikuyu: Kūgītīra: Eheria mahuti maria mathūku, tīgīthūkanīa wega mīmera na ūtīgīrīre maaī nimaraikūrūka tīri- inī wega

English: • Use of resistant cultivars e.g.) FHIA 17, 18, 25, some Cavendish varieties like Williams and Grand Nain
Kikuyu: Hūthīra mīmera īrīa īngītiria mīrimū ta FHIA 17,18,25 mīthemba ya Cavandish ta Williams na Grance Nain

English: • Use chemicals such as : – Mancozeb (DITHANE M-45®) – Thiophanate-Methyl (TOPSIN M Liquid®)
Kikuyu: Hūthīra dawa ta- Mancozeb( Dithane- m-45) - Thiophanate -Methyl ( Topsin M Liquid)

English: How to identify and manage Banana Bacterial Wilt
Kikuyu: ūngīmenya atīa na kūgitīra mūrimū wa Banana Bacterial wilt

English: Banana Bacterial Wilt
Kikuyu: Banana Bacterial wilt

English: General Descriptions:
Kikuyu: Mitugo yake:

English: • The disease is easily spread by use of infected planting materials and farm tools
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūtambaga ūngīhūthira īndo ciīna mūrimū ūyū

English: • Transmitted by insects including bees through the male bud
Kikuyu: Nīutambagio nī tūtambi ta njūkī harī kīro kīa njamba.

English: • Lacatan is a very susceptible variety Symptoms:
Kikuyu: Lacatan nīitiragia dariri ici

English: • Withering of flowers, wilting of leaves and premature ripening of fruits
Kikuyu: Kūhoha kwa mahūa na mahuti na kweruha matunda matakinyanīīre

English: • The leaf sheath turns dull green, scalded & breaks at petiole, then all leaves collapse at pseudo-stem
Kikuyu: Mahuti magacenjia kūrī rangi wa grini nogu, magatuīka na thutha-inī makambia kūgūa

English: • Cross section of the pseudo-stem when cut reveals yellow discoloration bacterial ooze
Kikuyu: Mūramba ungīrenguo ūrutaga rangi wa ngoikoni kana bakteria

English: • Un-even and premature ripening of fruits Control:
Kikuyu: Kūgītīra matunda kweruha matakinyanīīre na mataigananite:

English: • Field Sanitation:
Kikuyu: Kūrora wega mūgūnda waku

English: • Disinfecting farm tools after use and washing hands e.g.) use of jik 1:5 water
Kikuyu: Gūthambia wega indo cia mūgūnda na moko ūkīhūthīra dawa. Hūthīra jiki  na gīthimi kīa 1:5

English: • Uprooting, destroying, and burying affected plants
Kikuyu: Munya, thūkangia na ūthike mīmera īrīa īgūmīiruo

English: • Disbudding of male flower buds after fruiting
Kikuyu: Eheria kīro kīa njamba thutha wa kūruta matunda

English: • Observe quarantine
Kikuyu: Tīgīthūkania

English: • Use of clean planting materials
Kikuyu: Hūthīra indo therū cia kūhanda

English:
What would you consider the most dangerous disease of bananas at the moment, Xanthomonas
wilt or Fusarium wilt?
Kikuyu: Nī mīrīmū īrīkū mīūru makīria ya marigū rīu?  Xanthomonas wilt kana Fusarium wilt?

English: At the moment, Xanthomonas wilt is considered the most dangerous disease.
Kikuyu: Thaici, Xanthomonas wilt nīguo mūru makīria.

English: If banana has Mycosphaerella leaf spot, can it still produce bunches?
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo mahuti mena maroro ma Mycosphaerella, no īrute bunches

English: Yes, bananas that are infected with Mycosphaerella leaf spot still produce bunches.
Kikuyu: ona marigū magūmiruo nī mūrimu wa Mycosphaerella no marīciara.

English: However, the farmer should provide more manure to such bananas to increase the plant’s vigour and help it withstand the disease.
Kikuyu: Mūrīmi nī abatiī ahe mūmera thumu wa kūigana nīguo gūteithia mūmera kūrūma na wītirie mīrimū

English: Do bananas share any diseases with other crops like beans?
Kikuyu: marigū nīmanyitanagīra na mīmera īngī mīrimū ta mboco?

English: Some of the types of pathogens that cause disease in banana are also found in beans.
Kikuyu: Indo iria ikuaga mīrīmū marigū-ini harī marigū niyo īkūragio mboco ini

English: However, pathogens are generally rather specific, and diseases specific to banana do not occur on beans.
Kikuyu: Indo ici nī cia mwanya na mīrimū ya marigū nī ngūrani harī ya mboco

English: Can I grow bananas on sloping land in Kenya?
Kikuyu: No ngūrie marigū irīma- inī Kenya?

English: Yes, bananas can be grown on sloping land, but terracing and erosion control measures may be needed to prevent soil erosion.
Kikuyu: marigū nomakūrio irīma-inī, no he bata kūgitīra tīīrī ndūgakuo

English: How long does it take for banana plants to bear fruit after planting?
Kikuyu: marigū mayoga kahinda kaigana atīa gūciara matunda thutha wa kūhandwo?

English: Depending on the variety and growing conditions, bananas can start bearing fruit within 9 to 12 months after planting.
Kikuyu: O mūthemba na harīa mūmera ūhandītwo, marigū no mambie gūciara thutha wa mīeri 9-12

English: What is the average yield per īka for banana farming in Kenya?
Kikuyu: harī īka ya mūgūnda īciaraga marigū maigana atīa Kenya?

English: Yields can vary, but with proper management, an average yield of 20,000 to 30,000 kg (20 to 30 metric tons) per īka is achievable.
Kikuyu: Magetha nīmacenjagia, maramatītwo wega no maciare 20,000kg-30,000kg harī o īka

English: Is organic banana farming viable in Kenya?
Kikuyu: marigū ma kīmerera no marīmwo Kenya?

English: Yes, organic banana farming is viable and can fetch premium prices in the market, but it requires adherence to organic practices and certification.
Kikuyu: īī marigū ma kīmerera no magīe bei njega thoko inī no nīmabataraga ūrori wa kīmerera.

English: How to prepare land for planting bananas
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda wa marigū ūharagīrīrio atīa?

English: When planting in virgin land, start with clearing trees and shrubs on the new land.
Kikuyu: ūkīhanda mūgūnda mwerū, theria ithaka na mītī

English: Then spray the cleared land with broad spectrum herbicide to kill weeds like couch grass or just plough the land and harrow.
Kikuyu: Huhīra mūgūnda ūcio na dawa cīa ria kana urīme na ūhūre harū

English: Dig holes of size: 60cm x 60cm x 60cm  at spacing of 3m x 2m apart when establishing pure banana orchard.
Kikuyu: Eja marima ma 60cmx60cmx60cm na ūtīgithūkanie na 3mx2m angīkorwo nī mūgūnda wa marigū matheri.

English: Place the soil from the top 30 cm in a heap and the soil of the second 30 cm (subsoil) in a separate heap.
Kikuyu: Iga mwanya tīīrī wa igūrū wa 39cm kando na ucio ungi 30cm mwena

English: Obtain 20 kilogram of well decomposed manure for each hole, put the manure in hole and cover lightly with top soil.
Kikuyu: īkīra kilo 20 cia thumu mūhīu wega irima-inī na ūkunīke na gatīīrī kanini

English: Wait for 2 weeks and plant clean planting material (tissue culture) or paired corm of desired banana variety.
Kikuyu: Handa marigū maku thutha wa wiki īgīrī mūthemba ūria ūragūkenia

English: At planting, use phosphate fertilizer such as diamonium phosphate (DAP), single super phosphate (SSP) or (NPK 20, 20, 0), add 200 gram of the phosphate fertilizer to top soil and mix well with mature in the hole.
Kikuyu: ūkīhanda hūthīra bataraitha ya phosphate ta (DAP) Diamonium Phosphate , (SSP) Single super Phosphate kana (NPK 20:20:0) ongerera gramu 200 cia bataraitha ya phosphate harī tīīrī wa igūrū na ūtukanie wega na thumu irima-inī

English: Use 20 kilograms of chopped fresh tithonia leaves  in place of manure where necessary.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra kilo 20 cia mahuti ma Tithonia handu ha thumu

English: To have a good start for the banana plants, planting should be timed to coincide with the on-set of rains.
Kikuyu: Nigūo ūgīe na kīambīrīria kiega gīa mīmera tīgīrīra wa handa mbura īhakuhī kura

English: Which suckers is suitable for planting on banana farming
Kikuyu: Nī thuna irīkū ciagīrīire kūhandwo ūrīminī wa marigū?

English: Choose healthy sword sucker of superior banana cultivars and make sure there are no signs of weevil tunnels on the chosen suckers.
Kikuyu: Thura thuna iria njega itarī na marima ma mbūca

English: Remove all roots and a thin outer layer of corm skin epidermis this is known as paring.
Kikuyu: Eheria mīrī yothe na gakothi ka igūru- gūīka ūū gūitagwo paring

English: Place only the pared area of sucker in boiling water for 30 seconds or surface sterilized for 20 min in fungicide mixture.
Kikuyu: īkīra thuna hau harute ngothi maaī-ini mahiu rīa kahinda ga 30sec kana ūrinde kwa kahinda rīa 20sec

English: Remove the sterilized sucker and cut the top part to produce the corm.
Kikuyu: Ruta thuna īria irī na dawa na ūrenge gacunjī ka īgūrū

English: Plant the sterilized corm the same day.
Kikuyu: Hada gacunjī kau mūthenya ūcio

English: Mix 20 kilograms of well rotten manure with top soil and put the mixture in the hole.
Kikuyu: Tukania kilo 20 cia thumu ūhīte wega na tīīrī wa igūrū na ūtukanie wega irima-inī

English: Place the paired treated corm in the hole with the mixture.
Kikuyu: Iga mūmera waku irima-inī rīu

English: Use the sub-soil to fill up the hole and bury the corm in the hole at 10 cm below the soil cover.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra tīīrī wa thīinī kūihūria irima na ūthike mūmera na ūrikū wa sentimita 10 thīīnī wa tīīrī

English: How stablishment using tissue culture /macro propagated seedlings
Kikuyu: ūhadaga atīa marīgū ma mūciarithanio

English: Establishment using tissue culture /macro propagated seedlings.
Kikuyu: Kūhanda marigū ma mūciarithanio

English: When using tissue culture plantlets, mix 20 kilograms of well rotten manure with top soil and put the mixture in the hole.
Kikuyu: ūkīhuthira marigū ma mūciarithanio, tukania kilo 20 cia thumu ūhīte wega na tīīrī wa igūru na utukanie wega irima-inī

English: Remove the plastic bag container and place the TC plant, 30 cm deep in the hole with the mixture.
Kikuyu: Ruta karatathi, na wūige mūmera waku tīīrī-ini na ūriku wa sentimita 30 irīma- ini hamwe na matukanio ūcio

English: Use the sub-soil to fill up the hole to level of soil in pot.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra tīīrī wa gatagatī kūihūria irima

English: When planting without rains irrigate each hole with 20 litres of water.
Kikuyu: ūkīhanda rīrīa gūtarī mbura, itīrīria mīmera lita 20 cia maaī o irima

English: Land preparation for bananas
Kikuyu: Kūharīrīa mūgūnda wa marigū

English: Before planting, deep soil cultivation by ploughing and harrowing is recommended.
Kikuyu: ūtanahanda, kūrima ūrikītie kwendekithītio na ūcokerithie.

English: The fields should be free of trees, bushes and especially perennial weeds
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda ndūbatīī gūkoruo na mītī, ithaka na ria

English: What is the correct spacing for planting bananas?
Kikuyu: marigū mabatiī gūtigithūkanio atīa?

English: Should be done at the onset of rains.
Kikuyu: īka maūndū maya mbura īhakuhī kuura;

English:
Under dry conditions dig holes 90cm by 90cm by 60cm, in wet areas dig the holes 60cm by 60cm by 60cm.
Short variety (Dwarf Cavendish, Giant Cavendish) 2.5m × 3m.
Medium variety (Valery, Williams) 3.0m × 4.0m.
Tall variety (Lacatan, Poyo) 4.0m× 4.0m.
Kikuyu: Kūrī o kūmū, enja marima ma 90cmx90cmx60cm, no kūrī na ūgunyu, enja marima ma 60cmx60cmx60cm. Mīthemba īria mīkuhī ( dwarf Cavendish, Giant Cavendish) 2.5mx3m, mithemba ya ūraihu wa gatagatī-inī ( Valery, Williams) 3mx4m. Mīthemba mīraihu ( Lacatan,Poyo) 4.0mx4.0m

English: What is the best site to plant bananas?
Kikuyu: Nī handū harīkū hega ha kūhanda marigū?

English: Choose a location with suitable soil and climate conditions for banana cultivation.
Kikuyu: Thura handu harī na tīīrī mwega, riera riega ria kurīma marigū

English: Bananas prefer well-drained, loamy soil with good organic matter content.
Kikuyu: marigū mendaga tīīrī uraikūrūkia wega maaī na mūirū mūnoru

English: The site should receive sufficient sunlight and have access to water for irrigation.
Kikuyu: mūgūnda ūcio nī ūbatiī nī gūkinyīrwo rīua wega na ūkorwo hakuhī na maaī ma kūrīma.

English: How long does a banana take to mature in Kenya?
Kikuyu: marigū moyaga kahinda kaigana atīa gūkūra būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: The time for planting to maturity of a banana depends. on area and variety.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīa kūhanda nginya gūkūra kwa marīgū kūringanaga na harīa mahandītuo na mūthemba wamo.

English: A plant takes roughly 12 to 18 months to mature. Fruits are ready for harvesting 90 – 150 days after.
Kikuyu: Irigū rīoyaga kīndū mīeri 12-18 gūkūra. Marigū no magethuo thutha wa thīkū 90-150

English: What is the right spacing for banana?
Kikuyu: marigū mabatiī gūtigithūkanio atīa?

English: Experiments carried out recently show that good quality banana and heavy bunch can be achieved by keeping the planting distance at 1.8 X 1.8 m.
Kikuyu: īgerio iria ciīkītuo cionanagia īkīro njega cia marigū nīīkinyagīrwo mangītīgithokanio na ūraihu wa 1.8m x1.8m

English: However, to get maximum yield plantation is done at 1.2 X 1.5 m.
Kikuyu: Nigūo gūciara makīria tīgīthania na 1.2m x 1.5m

English:
High Density Planting :
High density planting is in practice to accommodate 4444 to 5555 plants per ha
Kikuyu: High density nī kūhanda itina 4444-5555 hactare-inī īmwe

English: Which month is best for banana plantation?
Kikuyu: Nī mweri ūrīkū mwega wa kūhanda marigū?

English: Bananas planted in March/April made better growth and gave higher yields than those planted upto December.
Kikuyu: marigū mahandituo mweri wa gatatū/kana nimakūraga wega na gūcīara wega makīria kūrī marīa mahandītuo thutha ūcio nginya Dithemba

English: Flowering occurred in less than 300 days after planting in March/April.
Kikuyu: Marigū marutaga kīro thutha wa thīkū 300 thutha wa kūhanda mwerī-inī wa gatatū/kana

English: Growth and flower bud differentiation and fruit development were adversely affected, when the average temperature was below 750 F
Kikuyu: Kūruta kīro na gūciara nīigomagīruo rīrīa ikīro cia rūgarī irī thīī wa 750F

English: Can you plant banana trees next to each other?
Kikuyu: No ūhande marīgū hakuhī na mītī īngī?

English: Keep at least 2 to 3 meter gap between 2 plants. Also keep good mulch around the base , so that new root growth should not start new shoots until you want them.
Kikuyu: Mītīgithūkanie na 2m -3m kuuma mūtī nginya irigū-inī. Tīgīrīra nī warīra mūcing'i gītina-inī nīguo mīrī mīerū ndīgathune nginya rīrīa ūkwnda.

English: Establishing a new banana garden?
Kikuyu: Uthondekaga atīa mūgūnda mweru wa marigū?

English: A site with deep, well-drained and fertile soils, preferably rich in organic matter is good for banana production.
Kikuyu: Marigū mekaga wega nandū harī na tīīrī mūriku, uraikūrūkia wega maaī na mūnoru

English: It will encourage the development of strong plants that can tolerate infections.
Kikuyu: Nīgūteithagia mūmera kūgia hinya na guītirīa mīrīmū

English: A newly opened land without signs or history of nematodes or the devastating Fusarium wilt and bacterial wilt diseases is preferred.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda mwerū ūtarī na mīnyongoro kana mīrimū mīūru ya kūhoha, Fusarium wilt na bacterial wilt, nīwendetwo

English: If the site has been used for production of bananas in the last two years, it is highly recommended to remove all remaining banana plants and corms.
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo mūgūnda ūcio nīwarīmītwo marigū mīaka īrī mīhītūku, nīwega weherio marigū marīa matiga hamwe na thuna

English: Normally such remnants harbour a lot of pests and diseases.
Kikuyu: Matigari mau mahithaga tūtambi na mīrimū.

English: The remnants should be transferred into another field (not of bananas), chopped and spread to dry or composted.
Kikuyu: Matigari mau mabatīe meherio mūgūnda-inī na matwaro kūngī, matinangio na manīkuo nīguo mome

English: The land should then be planted with a legume crop (like beans) or left to follow with a legume green manure cover crop for 1 to 2 years.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda ūcio nī ubatie ūhanduo mboco kana ūtiguo ūguo kwa ihinda rīa mwaka 1 nginya 2

English: This will ensure that any remaining pest or disease infections are completely removed before introducing new banana plants.
Kikuyu: Gūika ūū nīgūtigagīrīra tūtambi na mīrīmū yothe nīyeherio ūtanahada marigū merū.

English: All perennial weeds should also be removed and destroyed before planting because bananas are very susceptible to weed competition.
Kikuyu: Ria rīothe rībatiī rīeherio ūtanahada nīūndū marigū nīmatoragio nī ria

English: Some of the existing trees at the selected site should be left during land clearing in order to protect the young banana plants from wind and strong direct sunshine
Kikuyu: ūgītheria mūgūnda wa marigū tigīa mītī mīnini ya kūhinga rūhuho rūnene na rīua kūhe marigū kīruru marī manini.

English: What is banana tissue culture?
Kikuyu: Marīgu ma mūciarithanio nī marīkū?

English:
Tissue culture is a technique for multiplying clean banana planting material from small plant pieces
taken from a mother plant.
Kikuyu: īno nī njīra ya gūciarithania marīgū kuuma marigū-inī manini marutītwo kuuma kūrī mangī makūrū.

English: Although any part of the banana plant can be used to generate tissue culture plants, it is the meristem/shoot tip that is commonly used
Kikuyu: Ona angīkorūo gacunjī o gothe ka irigū no kahūthīke gūcīarithania nī thuna īhūthīkaga

English: How to produce tissue culture banana plantlets
Kikuyu: ūngīthondeka atīa marīgū ma mūciarithanio?

English:
1. Carefully select source materials (peepers or small sword suckers) that appear healthy (pest- and
disease-free) and that are growing vigorously. [Trainer: Show two suckers to the participants: one
that has visible evidence of pest or disease infestation and one that appears healthy. Explain that
you cannot be sure if you are planting infected or clean material, as most suckers appear to be
relatively healthy until their corms are exposed.]
Kikuyu: 1. Thura wega marigū maria ūgūciarithania matarī na mīrimū na tūtambi na marakūra narua. Onia arutuwo thuna ciothe, īmwe īrīa irī na mūrimū na īngī itarī. Matarīrie atī ndūngīkoruo na ūma kana nī mūrwaru kana ndūri mūrimu tondu thuna nyingī cionekaga īrī njega nginya rīrīa corms ikuoneka.

English: 2. Using either a hoe or a panga, separate the sucker (‘ex-plant’) from the mother plant.
Kikuyu: 2. ūkīhūthīra icembe kana banga, tīgīthūkanīa thuna iria irendeka na mūramba

English: 3. Clean the sucker by removing soil and cutting off the roots to expose the corm.
Kikuyu: 3. Thambia thuna ukīruta tīīri na ūtīnie mīrī nīgūo corms cioneke.

English: Cut off parts of the pseudostem to a length of about 30 cm
Kikuyu: Renga mūramba ūtigie sentimita 30.

English:
4. Pack the pseudostem parts in a clean nylon bag and transport them to a cleaner environment for
further manipulation.
Kikuyu: 4. Banga īcunjī icio cia mūramba karatathi-inī ka munyore gatheru na ūcithamie nginya handū hangī hatheru.

English: 5. Wash the sucker under tap water to remove mud and other debris.
Kikuyu: 5. Thambia thuna nīguo weherie ndoro na gīko kīngī.

English: 6. Trim away the outer leaf sheaths, leaf bases and corm tissue of the ex-plant until a 2 × 2 × 2 cm3 cube enclosing the shoot apex remains.
Kikuyu: 6. Eheria mathangū mothe manathī, na ūrenge mūrita wa corm ūtigīe ūrī na ithimo cia 2cmx2cmx2cm ūnyitanīte na mūthia wa thuna.

English: Be careful to avoid cutting through the apex.
Kikuyu: Menyerera ndūkarenge mūthia wa thuna

English: 7. The ex-plant is placed directly on a multiplication-inducing culture medium in containers.
Kikuyu: 7. iga mūmera ūcio wa mbere wagūciarithania thīīni wa tūmīkebe

English: A growth regulator (cytokinin) is added to the multiplication medium to speed up the process.
Kikuyu: Nigūo kūhanyūkia gūkūra nīyongagīrīruo cytokinin

English: The containers are transferred to incubators or growth chambers and maintained at an optimal growth temperature of 28 ± 2°C. 8.
Kikuyu: Mīkebe īyo īcoke īigwo thīinī wa incubators na ūrugarī wūigwo ūrī digirii 28 na ndūgakūre kana kūingīha na makīria ma digirii 2.

English: Banana tissue culture shoots often suffer from blackening caused by exudates from wounded tissues.
Kikuyu: mīthia ya thuna cia marigū ma mūciarithanio nī majitagwo nī mūrimū wa kūira nī iria kuuma guraro-inī.

English: These undesirable exudates form a barrier round the tissue, preventing nutrient uptake and hindering growth.
Kikuyu: Iria rīrī rītarendeka rīthondekaga thingo iria igiragia thuna kūria irio na igatūma ciīkururie mūkūrīre-inī.

English: To prevent this, during the first 4–6 weeks fresh shoots are transferred to new medium every 1–2 weeks.
Kikuyu: Nigūo kūgīrīrīria, wiki cia mbere 4-6, thuna njerū nīicenjagio thutha wa  wiki 1-2

English: 9. The shoot tips then start to develop.
Kikuyu: Thuna ici nacio  ikambīrīria  gūkūra

English: 10.Individual shoots or shoot clumps that have formed are transferred to a nutrient medium which does not promote further shoot proliferation but stimulates root formation (rooting media).
Kikuyu: Thuna kana ga kahīri gacio karīa gethondekete icokaga igathamīrio irio-inī itarareka thuna ingī ciīongerere no irateithia mīri īthondeke.(irio cia mīri)

English: After rooting, plantlets are ready to leave the laboratory for weaning and hardening.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa kūruta mīrī, mīmera īyo noīrutwo laboratory nīguo yūmīrīrie

English: Is it okay to establish a banana orchard using suckers derived from tissue culture plants?
Kikuyu: Ni wega kūiga natharī ya marigū ya thuna ciumanite na marigū ma mūciarithanio?

English:
Yes, it is ok and there are several people who sell suckers from orchards that were started using
tissue culture plants.
Kikuyu: īī no wūige,  nī kūri andu mendagia thuna icio ciumanite na marigū ma mūciarithanio

English: However, suckers derived from tissue culture plants are just like any other suckers, and not pest- and disease-free.
Kikuyu: Ona kūrī oūguo rī, thuna ciumanite na marigū ma mūciarithanio no ta thuna iria ingi kwoguo nīigumagīrwo nī mīrimū na tūtambi o ta thuna iria īngī

English: How long do tissue culture plants take to mature and fruit?
Kikuyu: Hihi marigū ma mūciarithanio maikaraga kahinda kaigana atīa gūkūra?

English:
If a tissue culture plant is managed very well, it will always fruit and mature faster than an
ordinary sucker.
Kikuyu: Mūmera wa gūcīarithanio ūngīrorwo wega no ūciare  na ūkūre narua gūkīra marigū ma kawaida ma thuna

English: Usually, this takes 9–12 months after planting.
Kikuyu: Kaingī ihūthagīra mīeri 9-12 itanahandwo

English: For how long does a banana field established using banana tissue plantlets last?
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda uhandītwo marigū ma mūciarithanio maikaraga kahinda kaigana atīa?

English:
How long a banana plantation lasts depends on the level of management, and is the same
whether started with tissue culture plantlets or other planting material.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda wa marigū ūhūthīkaga kahinda karaihu kūringana na ūria ūroretwo na nī ūndū ūmwe onakorwo ūhandītwo na njīra ngūrani.

English: A well-managed plantation should last for a minimum of 10 years.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda ūroretwo wega no wūikare mīaka ītanyihīīre ikūmi.

English: Are suckers from tissue culture plant also called tissue culture plants?
Kikuyu: Hihi thuna kuma mūti wa mūciarithanio no ciīrwo nī cia mūciarithanio?

English:
No. When a tissue culture plantlet is planted into the field, all the suckers it produces thereafter
are not tissue culture plants, they are ordinary suckers.
Kikuyu: Aca, marigū ma mūciarithanio mahandwo mūgūnda-inī, thuna īria īkūruta ti cia mūciarithanio nī thuna cia kawaida.

English: Can all banana cultivars be produced using banana tissue culture technology?
Kikuyu: marigū no makūrio na njīra ya mūciarithanio?

English: Yes, all banana cultivars can be produced using tissue culture technology.
Kikuyu: īī marigū no makūrio na njīra īno ya mūciarithanio

English: However, the management of a tissue culture laboratory will first assess the commercial potential of a demanded cultivar to ensure profits.
Kikuyu: Niguo gutungata marigū ma mūciarithanio, he bata kurora thoko ūrīa īhana nīguo gūtigīrīra baita nīkuoneka

English: What is the standard size of a planting hole?
Kikuyu: īrima rīa kūhanda marigū rīagīrīire kwenjwo rīigana atīa?

English: The size of the planting hole depends on many factors including soil type, soil texture, etc.
Kikuyu: Thaithi ya irima ūringanaga na maūndū ngūrani; mutheba wa tīīrī, ūnyoroku wa tīīrī hamwe na maundū mangī

English: It is common practice to dig a hole measuring 45 cm × 45 cm × 45 cm.
Kikuyu: Nī ūndū wīkīkaga kwenja irima rīīgana 45cmx45cmx45cm

English: If intercropping banana and coffee, when is it best to demarcate the planting holes for coffee?
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo nī kūhanda marigū na kahūa, nī īhinda rīrīku riega rīa gūthura harīa marīma ma kahūa makwenjwo?

English: It is best to plan and demarcate the planting holes for both coffee and banana at the same time.
Kikuyu: Nī wega kūgayūkania harīa marima ma kahūa na marigū makwenjwo hamwe

English: Should the planting holes be dug and manure applied on the same day?
Kikuyu: Nī wega kwenja marima na gūikīra thumu hamwe mūthenya ūmwe?

English: And can the bananas be planted on the same day?
Kikuyu: Na marigū no mahandwo mūthenya ūcio?

English: It is fine to dig the planting hole and apply manure at the same time.
Kikuyu: Hatirī thīna kwenja irima rīa kūhanda na gūikīra thumu hamwe

English: Whether you can also plant on the same day depends on the manure you are using.
Kikuyu: harī kūhanda mūthenya ūcio nī kūringanaga na mūthemba wa thumu

English: If the manure is very well dried and well mixed with the topsoil, then it is okay to plant on the same day
Kikuyu: Angīkoruo thumu ni mūniaru wega na ūgatukanio wega na tīri, ni wega kūhanda mūthenya ūcio

English: For how long should I water the banana plantlet after planting?
Kikuyu: Njagīrīire gūitīīrīria maaī kamūmera ka marigū thikū cīgana thutha wa kūhanda

English: The best practice is to water until the plantlet starts developing new leaves.
Kikuyu: wega nigūitīīrīria nginya mūmera wambie kūruta mathangū merī

English: However, you do not have to keep watering if it is raining sufficiently.
Kikuyu: Ona kūrī ūguo angīkorwo nī kūraura wega ndūbatīe gūitīīrīria maaī rīngī

English: What is the significance of applying mulch around the newly planted bananas?
Kikuyu: He bata ūrīku wa kwarīra marigū manini?

English: This is done to prevent water loss.
Kikuyu: Gwika ūguo nīguo kūgirīrīria kūra kwa maaī

English: As the plantlets are fragile immediately after planting, if they dry out they can become damaged or die.
Kikuyu: Tondū mīmera īno nī minini no yūme, īthūke kana īkue

English:
When is the best time to plant the bananas – is it in the middle of the rainy season or at the
onset?
Kikuyu: Nī ihinda rīrīku rīega rīa kūhanda marigū- nī gatagatīini wa mbura kana itambītie?

English: The best time to plant bananas is at the onset of the rainy season.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīega makīrīa rīa kūhanda marigū nī rīrīa mbura īhakuhī kura

English: This means that you have to have the field prepared (planting holes dug and manure applied into the holes) before the rains start.
Kikuyu: ūū nī kuuga no nginya ūharīrīrie mūgūnda mbere (kwenja marima na gūīkīra thumu marīma-ini) mbura ītaurīte.

English: Would you recommend intercropping banana and maize?
Kikuyu: No wendekīthie kūhanda mbembe na marigū hamwe?

English: It is not recommended to intercrop maize and banana since maize will compete with bananas for light.
Kikuyu: Ndūtarītuo kūhanda mbembe na marigū hamwe tondū nīitharanaga ūtheri wa rīua.

English: Also, unlike crops that fix nitrogen in the soil, for example beans, maize does not add any nutrients into the soil.
Kikuyu: Mbembe ititeithagia kuongerera nitrogen tīīrī-inī ūndū ūria mboco ciakaga.

English: Why is it that in the past (20 years ago) banana fields lasted longer than they do today?
Kikuyu: Nīkīi gīatūmaga miaka 20 mīhetūku mīgūnda ya marīgū yaikaraga kahinda karaihu gūkīra rīu?

English:
There could be several reasons to explain why banana fields used to last longer in the past, such as less disease pressure (e.g. diseases like Xanthomonas wilt were not yet present), soil fertility was
not as depleted as it is currently, and global warming was not as intense as it is now.
Kikuyu: Nogūkoruo na itūmi nyingī ingītwīra nīkīī mūgūnda wa marigū wahūthīkaga kahinda karaihu mīaka ya thutha; mīrīmū yarī mīnini, (mūrimū ta Xanthomonas wilt ndīarīkuo) tīīrī warī mūnoru gukīra rīu na ūrūgarī dwarī mūingi.

English: But if you take care of your banana field it should last for 10 years.
Kikuyu: No angīkoruo nīūgutungata mūgūnda waku, marigū moyaga mīaka ikūmī

English: Can bananas be grown in swampy areas?
Kikuyu: marigū no mahanduo gītuamba-inī kīri na maaī?

English: Yes, they can be grown in swampy areas, as long as the soil is not waterlogged for long periods.
Kikuyu: īī, no mahanduo gītuamba-inī angīkoruo tīīrī ndūgūikara na maaī kahinda karaihu

English: What are the key steps in post-harvest handling of bananas?
Kikuyu: Nī makinya marīkū ubatīe kūrūmīriīra thutha wa kūgetha?

English: Post-harvest handling includes careful harvesting, proper packaging, and storage in a cool, well-ventilated area to extend shelf life.
Kikuyu: ūkīgetha, getha ūbacīrīīre, banga na ūige marīgū maku handū harī rīera rīracera wega nīguo maikare kahinda karaihu.

English: How far apart should I space banana plants in my plantation?
Kikuyu: Batīe gūtigithūkania atīa mīmera ya marīgū mūgūnda-inī wakwa?

English: The recommended spacing is usually 2.5 meters between rows and 2 meters between individual banana plants. Some varieties require 3 x 3 or 3 x 2m.
Kikuyu: Gūtigithūkanio kūrīa kwendekithītio nī kwa mita 2.5 kuuma laini nginya īngī na mita 2 kuuma mūmera-inī ūmwe nginya ūrīa ūngī. Nī harī mīthemba ībataraga 3mx3m kana 3mx2m

English: What would you recommend and why: cooking or roasting types?
Kikuyu: Ūngīendekithia atīa na nīkī: Kūruga kana gūcina?

English: This totally depends on the farmer’s interest.
Kikuyu: Ūū kūraringana na wendi wa mūrīmi

English: The management practices are the same.
Kikuyu: Ūtungata nī ūhanaine

English: What are the common pests that affect banana plants in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī tūtambi tūrīkū twa marigū tūīkaine makīrīa būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: Common banana pests in Kenya include banana weevils, nematodes, aphids, thrips, and spider mites.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi twa marigū būrūri-inī wa Kenya nī hamwe na mboca cia marigū, mīnyongoro, ūme, thrips na mbumbuī.

English: Are there banana varieties resistant to Black Sigatoka in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīthemba ya marigū nīītiragia mūrimū wa Black Sigatoka būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: Yes, some banana varieties, such as FHIA-17 and FHIA-23, have shown resistance to Black Sigatoka.
Kikuyu: īī, mīthemba ya marigū ta FHIA-17 na FHIA-23, yonanītie gwitīria na Black Sigatoka.

English: How often should I irrigate my banana plantation?
Kikuyu: Hihi batīī gūitīīrīria marigū maaī maita maigana?

English: The frequency of irrigation depends on local conditions, but bananas generally require regular watering, especially during dry periods.
Kikuyu: Maita marīa ūngīitīīrīrīa maaī kūringanaga na maundu matiganīte, o na kūri ūguo, marigū nīmabataraga maaī kaingī makīria hīndī ya riūa.

English: Would you recommend a farmer to water his or her banana plantation even after mulching?
Kikuyu: Hihi no wendekīthie harī mūrīmi gūītīīrīrīa maaī mūgūnda wake angīkoruo niarīīre mūcing'i?

English: Yes, it important to water the plantation even after mulching (unless there is plenty of rain).
Kikuyu: nī harī bata gūītīīrīria maaī ona ngīkorūo nī warīire mūcing'i. (no gūkorirwo mbura nyingī nīīraura)

English: Mulching does not add water to the soils but only conserves water which is already there by reducing the rate at which the soil loses water to the atmosphere.
Kikuyu: Kwarīra gūtiongagīrīra maaī tīīri-inī no nīkūgītīra marīa marī tīīri-inī matikore rīera-inī
